data.body:
Session summary (2026-06-24). Five workstreams:

1) LEAN LIST OUTPUT (shipped, PR #14 MERGED -> main ba920b19). `rebar list` + MCP `list_tickets` now omit
   description/comments by default (open-epics output 152KB -> 14.5KB); `--full` / `full=True` opt-in.
   Mechanism: `list_states(..., include_body=True)`; library `rebar.list_tickets(full=True)` default-full so
   internal callers unaffected. Reconciler `_read_local_tickets` now shells `rebar list --full` (it diffs
   bodies vs Jira; lean read caused spurious mutations — caught by test_reconcile_idempotency). Tests updated.
   Tracked: over-ichor-spear (closed, signed).

2) ERROR-HANDLING EPIC (ring-gun-jot, reframed + 8 children created). Brainstormed via joe-brainstorm.
   Key decisions, grounded in peer research (basic-memory, modelcontextprotocol/servers reference servers,
   mcp-atlassian, MCP SDK/FastMCP): (a) REMEDIATION-LED, not a decision-doc — convention lives in an exemplar
   PR + lint gate. (b) DISCIPLINE-ONLY convention, no shared best-effort helper (peers don't have one): MCP
   surface just raises (framework -> isError), CLI base-error -> exit code, library named-logger + NullHandler,
   best-effort = inline narrow-and-log. (c) BLE001 + scoped T201 ON in CI (architectural enforcement), shrinking
   per-file-ignore allowlist (module-size-gate pattern). (d) Terminal state = OBSERVABILITY FLOOR (log exc_info
   OR justified noqa; narrowing opportunistic). (e) stdout is sacred (CLI data contract + MCP framing).
   8 children, sequenced substrate -> exemplar(plan_review) -> 5 sweeps -> reconciler(late) -> close-out;
   file-impact set on each. Aliases: slip-clink-conch, sweet-patch-son, crow-aeon-muck, eager-swab-swoop,
   dump-dingo-beast, drippy-tabby-chapel, colder-bugle-rake, colder-vista-rod.

3) FOUR-PASS DOC FIX (bold-coach-quail, PR #15 open + auto-merge). The plan-review gate runs FOUR passes
   (find->verify->decide->coach) but surfaces said "three-pass ... advisory coaching review", undercounting
   Pass-4. Corrected gate-level descriptions in passes.py, mcp_server.py, _cli/_llm_commands.py, the verdict
   schema, plan_review/__init__.py, attest.py, docs/plan-review-gate.md, docs/reuse-surface.md. Preserved the
   'shared three-pass framework (epic 9da1)' lineage and the drift-gated reviewer prompts. 382 tests pass.

4) JOE-DEBUG on 'review-plan didn't complete'. Root cause: MISDIAGNOSIS, not a defect. The review actually
   COMPLETED (PASS, 0 blocking/10 advisory, signed attestation on ring-gun-jot). The 'killed mid-pipeline'
   call was an artifact of `... 2>&1 | tail -40` buffering all output until EOF (file stayed 0 bytes during the
   multi-hour run) + a premature 'process exited' read + misreading the Pass-1-only cache as 'stopped after
   Pass 1' (passes 2-4 don't write per-chunk cache). No code fix. RESIDUAL PERF CONCERN: wall-clock ~hours vs
   docs' 'seconds to minutes' — unexplained, worth a separate look. Then verified the 10 advisories vs code and
   remediated the 4 VALID ones into the tickets: (G4) added _io/ (7 BLE001 noqas in import_ndjson.py) to the
   core-leaves sweep + file-impact (closes allowlist-reachability gap); (G4) substrate now wires a stderr
   handler at rebar_reconciler/__main__.main + NullHandler on the SIBLING 'rebar_reconciler' root logger (not
   under 'rebar'); (G4) corrected T201 scope (all 42 T201 noqas are in _engine/rebar_reconciler/, not the CLI);
   (T5e) added a 'document the convention in CLAUDE.md' deliverable + softened AC4 (F1, was vacuously
   satisfiable). RULED OUT as invalid: the 'ERROR: grep contract' finding (no test greps reconciler stderr).

5) CHILD-EPIC CONSISTENCY: updated close-out (colder-vista-rod) to own the softened epic AC4 (record whether
   any narrowing surfaced a bug) and fixed a stale 'CLI data prints' T201 AC line.

LOOSE ENDS: (a) rebar.toml has a typo'd key `verify.require_plan_review_for_claim` (warned/ignored on every
command) — verify intended key per docs/config.md. (b) The 8 ring-gun-jot children still need `rebar
review-plan` before any claim (plan-review gate); only the epic has been reviewed/signed so far. (c) Perf:
investigate why an epic review-plan took ~hours.
